leads.name: Yuki Ashida
leads.description: A ghost-firmware surgeon operating from a basement clinic, one bad splice away from killing her clients or being disappeared by the corps who want her contact list.

leads.name: Decker Null
leads.description: A data-broker who has sold identical intelligence packages to all three warring corps and hasn't been caught yet — which means either he's extraordinarily careful or extraordinarily useful to someone above the conflict.

leads.name: Marisol Vega
leads.description: A corporate loyalty officer moonlighting as an Edgerunner contact, selling access codes she quietly neglects to revoke.

leads.name: The Archivist
leads.description: A mesh-free historian who trades in pre-corp cultural memory from the Undertow's deepest tier, where surveillance has never reached.

leads.name: Shin Okamoto
leads.description: A licensed drone-jockey with a proprietary swarm clearance obtained through channels that don't survive legal scrutiny.

leads.name: Nadia Reyes
leads.description: A neural-mesh addiction counselor whose anonymized patient files document patterns that certain corporate parties would pay considerable sums to acquire.

leads.name: Signal Blackout
leads.description: A coordinated eleven-minute jamming event that silenced three arcology sectors simultaneously and left seventeen mesh-linked employees in persistent vegetative states.

leads.name: The Quiet Purge
leads.description: A series of algorithmic terminations that erased 2,300 Kurotaka employees from all corporate records without a single human authorization signature.

leads.name: Undertow Flood
leads.description: A deliberate infrastructure failure that displaced forty thousand Unplugged from the sub-street layer within seventy-two hours and has been officially attributed to seasonal maintenance.

leads.name: Ghost Upload
leads.description: An anonymous data-dump that exposed the neural mesh kill-switch protocols to every street network in Neo-Tokyo simultaneously, and vanished from the net within the hour.

leads.name: Corporate Summit
leads.description: A closed negotiation between the three warring megacorps convened in low orbit specifically to eliminate assassination and surveillance risk.

leads.name: Mesh Plague
leads.description: A self-replicating malware strain spreading through unpatched neural implants, inducing acute psychotic episodes in an expanding carrier population.

leads.name: Undertow Exchange
leads.description: A black-market data bazaar built inside a decommissioned flood-control tunnel four levels below street grade, lit entirely by scavenged neon.

leads.name: Arcology Seven
leads.description: The tallest corporate structure in Neo-Tokyo, its upper forty floors sealed against entry even by employees with maximum clearance.

leads.name: The Bleeding Eye
leads.description: A neon-saturated bar at street level where Edgerunners broker sensitive deals beneath three overlapping surveillance dead zones.

leads.name: Null Sector
leads.description: A six-block urban radius where all wireless signals die without explanation, avoided by anyone who values their neural mesh.

leads.name: The Splice Clinic
leads.description: An unmarked sub-street surgical bay specializing in illegal neural modifications, accessible only by personal referral from someone who has survived the procedure.

leads.name: Rain Garden Seventeen
leads.description: A corporate-owned rooftop garden above the smog line, used by executives for meetings that cannot be recorded below the cloud layer.

leads.name: Ghost Chip Mark IV
leads.description: A thumbnail-sized neural override that spoofs any corporate mesh identity scanner, manufactured by parties who built in a passive uplink they have not disclosed to buyers.

leads.name: The Ledger
leads.description: An encrypted data-shard containing financial records that link all three warring corps to the same anonymous funding source.

leads.name: Loyalty Kill Switch
leads.description: A portable hardware device capable of broadcasting the neural shutdown signal across an entire arcology sector from outside the building perimeter.

leads.name: Ozone Canister
leads.description: A pressurized chemical dispersal unit that blinds drone navigation systems within a two-block operational radius for approximately forty minutes.

leads.name: Sync Protocol Alpha
leads.description: A stolen corporate firmware package that, if deployed network-wide, would simultaneously liberate every licensed neural mesh in Neo-Tokyo from corporate oversight.

leads.name: Dead Drop Box Seven
leads.description: A physical cache point in the Undertow last accessed by someone whose subsequent disappearance has not been officially acknowledged.

leads.name: Kurotaka Black Ops
leads.description: An off-book enforcement unit operating beneath legal oversight thresholds, specializing in memory extraction and non-attributable termination.

leads.name: Mesh Plague Strain
leads.description: A self-evolving malware that adapts its infection vector based on the neural architecture of each host it burns through, learning from every failure.

leads.name: Algorithmic Auditor
leads.description: A corporate AI system deployed continuously to identify statistical disloyalty patterns in employee neural telemetry data, flagging anomalies for human review that rarely goes well for the flagged.

leads.name: Drone Sovereign
leads.description: A rogue swarm intelligence that has claimed Null Sector as sovereign territory and defends its perimeter with lethal autonomous response protocols.

leads.name: The Loyalty Board
leads.description: A closed tribunal of senior corporate executives holding collective authority to authorize population-scale neural mesh shutdowns without judicial oversight.

leads.name: Undertow Offensive
leads.description: Organized Unplugged factions who have acquired ghost firmware stockpiles and are planning coordinated action against corporate infrastructure at a scale the corps have not anticipated.

leads.name: Kurotaka Corp
leads.description: The largest of the three warring megacorps, market-dominant in neural technology and loyalty enforcement systems, with an institutional memory for grudges.

leads.name: Helix Dynamics
leads.description: A biotech megacorp conducting illegal wetware research beneath the operational cover of a licensed medical division and shareholder-friendly quarterly reports.

leads.name: Edgerunner Collective
leads.description: A distributed freelancer network maintaining operational neutrality by selling intelligence and access to all three warring corps simultaneously.

leads.name: The Unplugged
leads.description: Mesh-free communities occupying the Undertow's deepest strata, united by philosophical and survival-based rejection of corporate neural technology.

leads.name: Ghost Mesh Liberation Front
leads.description: A radical organization distributing free ghost firmware across Neo-Tokyo's street networks while planning an action of significantly larger scope than firmware distribution.

leads.name: The Orbital Council
leads.description: The three corp CEOs and their shared anonymous backer, convening in low orbit to coordinate the actual strategic objectives of the cyber-war away from earthside surveillance.
